page: Amazon Web Services x2gd.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the custom 64-bit ARM-based AWS Graviton2 processor. It features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz on the Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture, with no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core. It includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 72810.23. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff; the instance exhibits weaker single-threaded capabilities, with a geekbench single-core score of 955.0 and poor single-threaded bzip3 decompression rates. Memory bandwidth remains average, with read-only large block speeds at 144.33 GB/sec and write speeds at 71.96 GB/sec. LLM inference speeds are moderate for smaller models but drop significantly for larger 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides high memory density and dedicated physical cores without the overhead of hyperthreading, offering a favorable performance-per-resource profile for memory-bound workloads. The combination of 768 GB of RAM and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage makes this instance highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, caching layers like Redis, and memory-resident data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores powered by the AWS Graviton2 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is provisioned with 768.0 GB of RAM, offering a high 16:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio, alongside 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, though single-core execution and memory latency present performance tradeoffs. The instance provides high resource density and cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, and in-memory analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 48 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 768 GB RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage, built for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 48 Graviton2 cores, 768 GB RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring high memory density, dedicated physical cores, and local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated physical ARM64 cores running at 2.5 GHz with no hyperthreading
768 GB of system memory providing a high 16 GB per core ratio
2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage for low-latency data access
Strong multi-core CPU performance balanced by weaker single-threaded execution
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and in-memory analytics
